author: han0110
content: <@412614104222531604> Is there a way in kurtosis to inspect the label of a docker image when planning? I'm thinking to attach the ere version as label of zkboost image so it can auto resolve (if not I'd fallback to use `read_file` to fetch and parse the `Cagro.toml` in zkboost repo as a workaround)

author: taulepton_
content: is the label part of the image definition?

author: taulepton_
content: maybe you can parse it

author: taulepton_
content:
```
  zkvms:
    - kind: ere
      proof_type: reth-zisk
      image: ghcr.io/eth-act/ere/ere-server-zisk:0.6.1-cuda
      program_url: https://github.com/eth-act/ere-guests/releases/download/v0.7.0/stateless-validator-reth-zisk
      port: 3000
```

`zkvms.image.split(":")[1]`?

author: han0110
content: ah I mean given a `zkboost` image, we can use `docker buildx imagetools inspect` to insepct the label to resolve the tag for the `ere-server-zisk`, so we don't need to specify it in the yaml file

author: taulepton_
content: Ahhh right I see that makes sense

author: han0110
content:
because each zkboost is built with specific version of `ere` (server) and `ere-guests` (guest program) so both version can be set as label in zkboost image.

so the minimum gpu config will look like:

```yaml
additional_services:
  - zkboost
zkboost_params:
  zkvms:
    - kind: ere
      proof_type: reth-zisk
      gpu:
        device_ids: ["0", "1", "2", "3"]
    - kind: ere
      proof_type: ethrex-zisk
      gpu:
        device_ids: ["4", "5", "6", "7"]
```

where the shared memory and ulimit will be auto set if it's zisk (others don't need), and the `image` and `progam_url` will be auto resolved if no override

author: han0110
content: And I assume the minimum config (no `zkvms` configured) will spin up some mock zkvms instead of gpu ones?
